content.thinking: Let me start by reviewing the PR using the GitHub CLI and then look at the diff to understand what changes were made.

{"additions":2431,"body":"## Summary

Three changes to close the loop between a junior-dev run and the Jira ticket it implements:

- **Commit prefix** — every agent commit message now leads with the ticket key (`KAN-42 add foo helper`) so the GitHub for Jira integration surfaces commits in the issue's Development panel.
- **End-of-run Jira comment + transition** — after `gh pr create` succeeds the agent posts a single comment on the ticket with the PR URL, then transitions the status from **In Progress** → **In Review**. Surfaces the PR for human review and moves the ticket out of the In Progress column.
- **Allowlist expansion** — `--allowedTools` now includes `jira issue comment add` and `jira issue move` (and their `bin/` variants), scoped tightly. No other mutating jira commands are reachable.

Also re-applies the **Opus 4.7 model pin** (`--model claude-opus-4-7`) which was lost in the squash-merge of #35 (it landed after the merge happened).

## Files

- `agents/junior-dev/prompt.md` — commit-message convention; new "Finishing" closing block (comment + transition); Forbidden updated to allow exactly one comment + the In Review transition.
- `agents/junior-dev/README.md` — new "Jira ↔ GitHub linkage" section.
- `.github/workflows/junior-dev.yml` — `--allowedTools` expanded; `--model claude-opus-4-7` re-applied.

## Test plan

- [ ] **Verify linkage** — after first dispatch, open KAN-X in Jira; Development panel should show the branch, the commit(s), and the open PR. Comment thread should have one entry: "Implementation ready for review: <URL>".
- [ ] **Verify transition** — KAN-X status should be **In Review** after a successful run.
- [ ] **Verify graceful failure** — if the project's workflow doesn't permit the In Review transition from In Progress, the agent should log the error and stop without retrying. Run log should still commit.
- [ ] **Verify Opus pin** — `grep claude-opus-4-7 .github/agent-logs/junior-dev/<DATE>/<RUN_ID>.json` should match (the model name appears in the SDK response metadata).

## Notes

- The agent uses `jira issue comment add` (not Atlassian smart-commit `#comment` syntax). Smart-commits depend on the committer's email matching a Jira user, which the bot's `github-actions[bot]` email doesn't. Direct CLI mutation is more reliable.
- The transition is hard-coded to **In Review**. If your project's workflow names the column differently, edit `agents/junior-dev/prompt.md` — it's the only place the literal string lives.

1	You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
2	prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
3	out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
4	and open a Draft PR. Then stop.
5	
6	## Environment
7	
8	- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
9	  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
10	- `jira issue view` for reading; `jira issue comment add` and
11	  `jira issue move` only at the end of the run (see *Finishing*).
12	  Never call any other mutating jira command. Always pass `--no-input`
13	  on mutating jira calls — the CLI is interactive by default.
14	- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
15	  branches; don't force-push.
16	
17	## Workflow
18	
19	1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
20	   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
21	2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
22	   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
23	3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
24	   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
25	4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
26	   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
27	   plausible to run.
28	5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. **Lead
29	   every commit message with the ticket key**, e.g.
30	   `git commit -m "${TICKET_KEY} add foo helper"`. This surfaces the
31	   commit in the Jira issue's Development panel. Small focused commits
32	   or a single squash-style commit are both fine. Don't commit broken
33	   code.
34	6. Push, open PR, then close out on Jira (below).
35	
36	## Finishing
37	
38	```sh
39	git push -u origin HEAD
40	gh pr create --draft \
41	  --base main \
42	  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
43	  --body "<body>"
44	```
45	
46	If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
47	the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.
48	
49	**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:
50	
51	- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
52	- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
53	  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
54	- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
55	  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
56	  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
57	- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.
58	
59	Once `gh pr create` returns, capture the PR URL and close out on Jira:
60	
61	```sh
62	PR_URL="<the URL gh pr create printed>"
63	jira issue comment add "$TICKET_KEY" "Implementation ready for review: $PR_URL"
64	jira issue move "$TICKET_KEY" "In Review"
65	```
66	
67	Run both — the comment first, then the transition. Do this even on
68	`[WIP]` runs (the PR still needs human attention). If either jira
69	command fails (e.g. "In Review" isn't a valid transition from the
70	current status), note the error in your final output but don't retry
71	in a loop. Then stop. Output no further text.
72	
73	## Forbidden
74	
75	- Editing the Jira ticket description.
76	- Transitioning the ticket to anything other than **In Review** (no
77	  Done, no Blocked, no other status).
78	- Adding more than one comment to the ticket — exactly one PR-link
79	  comment at the end of the run.
80	- Modifying other agents' prompts or `.github/workflows/*.yml`.
81	- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
82	- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
83	
84	## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible
85	
86	Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
87	take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
88	explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

```sh
  --body "<body>"
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

Once `gh pr create` returns, capture the PR URL and close out on Jira:

```sh
PR_URL="<the URL gh pr create printed>"
jira issue comment add "$TICKET_KEY" "Implementation ready for review: $PR_URL"
jira issue move "$TICKET_KEY" "In Review"
```

Run both — the comment first, then the transition. Do this even on
`[WIP]` runs (the PR still needs human attention). If either jira
command fails (e.g. "In Review" isn't a valid transition from the
current status), note the error in your final output but don't retry
in a loop. Then stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket description.
- Transitioning the ticket to anything other than **In Review** (no
  Done, no Blocked, no other status).
- Adding more than one comment to the ticket — exactly one PR-link
  comment at the end of the run.
- Modifying other agents' prompts or `.github/workflows/*.yml`.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

file.content:
name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '60'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --model claude-opus-4-7 \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(jira issue comment add:*),Bash(bin/jira issue comment add:*),Bash(jira issue move:*),Bash(bin/jira issue move:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

This inconsistency could cause the LLM agent to run the commands without `--no-input`, which would cause them to hang waiting for interactive input in CI.

2. **WIP comment/transition should probably not happen** - Wait, the PR description says: "Do this even on `[WIP]` runs (the PR still needs human attention)." The prompt says: "Run both — the comment first, then the transition. Do this even on `[WIP]` runs." If it's a WIP run (e.g., due to a turn cap or blocker), transitioning to "In Review

" might be misleading since the implementation isn't complete. But this is a design choice, not a bug.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Download a pinned jira-cli binary into ./bin/, no brew or system install
# required. Re-runnable: skips work if the right version is already there.
#
# Run from anywhere: `bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh`.

set -euo pipefail

JIRA_CLI_VERSION="1.7.0"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BIN_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/bin"
# Real binary lives behind a wrapper at $BIN_DIR/jira, which auto-sets
# JIRA_CONFIG_FILE to this repo's .jira/config.yml.
BIN_PATH="$BIN_DIR/.jira-bin"
WRAPPER_PATH="$BIN_DIR/jira"

err() { printf >&2 'error: %s\n' "$*"; }
note() { printf '%s\n' "$*"; }

write_wrapper() {
  cat > "$WRAPPER_PATH" <<'WRAPPER'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Wrapper around the real jira-cli binary at bin/.jira-bin.
#
# Auto-injects JIRA_CONFIG_FILE pointing at this repo's .jira/config.yml so
# `jira` uses the per-clone config instead of ~/.config/.jira/.config.yml,
# which jira-cli would otherwise pick up by default. An explicit JIRA_CONFIG_FILE
# in the environment, or a -c/--config flag passed through, still wins.
#
# Regenerated by scripts/install-jira-cli.sh — edits here will be overwritten.

set -e

WRAPPER_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$WRAPPER_DIR/.." && pwd)"
REAL_BIN="$WRAPPER_DIR/.jira-bin"
PROJECT_CONFIG="$REPO_ROOT/.jira/config.yml"

if [[ ! -x "$REAL_BIN" ]]; then
  printf >&2 'error: %s not found. Run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh\n' "$REAL_BIN"
  exit 1
fi

if [[ -z "${JIRA_CONFIG_FILE:-}" && -f "$PROJECT_CONFIG" ]]; then
  export JIRA_CONFIG_FILE="$PROJECT_CONFIG"
fi

exec "$REAL_BIN" "$@"
WRAPPER
  chmod +x "$WRAPPER_PATH"
}

uname_s="$(uname -s)"
uname_m="$(uname -m)"

case "$uname_s" in
  Darwin) os="macOS" ;;
  Linux)  os="linux" ;;
  *) err "unsupported OS: $uname_s"; exit 1 ;;
esac

case "$uname_m" in
  arm64|aarch64) arch="arm64" ;;
  x86_64|amd64)  arch="x86_64" ;;
  *) err "unsupported arch: $uname_m"; exit 1 ;;
esac

asset="jira_${JIRA_CLI_VERSION}_${os}_${arch}.tar.gz"
url="https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/releases/download/v${JIRA_CLI_VERSION}/${asset}"
checksums_url="https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/releases/download/v${JIRA_CLI_VERSION}/checksums.txt"

if [[ -x "$BIN_PATH" ]]; then
  current="$("$BIN_PATH" version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'Version="[^"]+"' | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')"
  if [[ "$current" == "$JIRA_CLI_VERSION" ]]; then
    note "jira-cli v$JIRA_CLI_VERSION already present at $BIN_PATH"
    write_wrapper
    note "refreshed wrapper at $WRAPPER_PATH"
    exit 0
  fi
  note "found jira-cli v$current at $BIN_PATH; replacing with v$JIRA_CLI_VERSION"
fi

mkdir -p "$BIN_DIR"
tmp="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmp"' EXIT

note "downloading $asset..."
curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$tmp/$asset"
curl -fsSL "$checksums_url" -o "$tmp/checksums.txt"

note "verifying checksum..."
expected="$(grep " $asset\$" "$tmp/checksums.txt" | awk '{print $1}')"
if [[ -z "$expected" ]]; then
  err "no checksum entry for $asset in checksums.txt"
  exit 1
fi
if command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  actual="$(shasum -a 256 "$tmp/$asset" | awk '{print $1}')"
elif command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  actual="$(sha256sum "$tmp/$asset" | awk '{print $1}')"
else
  err "neither shasum nor sha256sum available; cannot verify download"
  exit 1
fi
if [[ "$actual" != "$expected" ]]; then
  err "checksum mismatch: expected $expected, got $actual"
  exit 1
fi

note "extracting..."
tar -xzf "$tmp/$asset" -C "$tmp"

# Tarball layout: jira_<version>_<os>_<arch>/bin/jira
extracted_bin="$(find "$tmp" -type f -name jira -path '*/bin/jira' | head -1)"
if [[ -z "$extracted_bin" ]]; then
  err "could not locate jira binary in extracted archive"
  exit 1
fi

mv "$extracted_bin" "$BIN_PATH"
chmod +x "$BIN_PATH"

write_wrapper

note "installed jira-cli v$JIRA_CLI_VERSION → $BIN_PATH"
note "wrote wrapper → $WRAPPER_PATH"
note ""
note "Verify: $WRAPPER_PATH version"

content.text:
### Move/Transition issue

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Transition an issue from one state to another. Supports interactive prompts, passing parameters directly, and adding comments, resolutions, or assignees during the transition.

```sh
# Move an issue using an interactive prompt
$ jira issue move

# Pass required parameters to skip prompt
$ jira issue move ISSUE-1 "In Progress"

# Move an issue and add comment
$ jira issue move ISSUE-1 "In Progress" --comment "Started working on it"

# Set resolution to fixed and assign to self while moving the issue
$ jira issue move ISSUE-1 Done -RFixed -a$(jira me)
```

--------------------------------

### Edit issue

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Edit an issue with various options including summary, priority, labels, components, and description. The `--no-input` option disables interactive prompts. Labels, components, and fixVersions can be added or removed.

```sh
# Edit issue in the configured project
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New Bug" -yHigh -lbug -lurgent -CBackend -b"Bug description"

# Use --no-input option to disable interactive prompt
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 -s"New updated summary" --no-input

# Use minus (-) to remove label, component or fixVersion
# For instance, to edit issue to
#   - remove label p2, component FE, fixVersion v1.0
#   - add label p1, component BE, fixVersion v2.0
$ jira issue edit ISSUE-1 --label -p2 --label p1 --component -FE --component BE --fix-version -v1.0 --fix-version v2.0
```

--------------------------------

### Add a worklog with a comment

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Adds a worklog to a Jira issue with a specified comment and skips the interactive prompt.

```sh
# You can add a comment using --comment flag when adding a worklog
$ jira issue worklog add ISSUE-1 "10m" --comment "This is a comment" --no-input
```

--------------------------------

### Add comment to issue

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

Add a comment to an issue. Supports both Github-flavored and Jira-flavored markdown, internal comments, and loading pre-defined templates. Supports interactive prompts or passing parameters directly.

```sh
# Add a comment using an interactive prompt
$ jira issue comment add

# Pass required parameters to skip prompt
$ jira issue comment add ISSUE-1 "My comment body"

# Same as above but as an internal comment
$ jira issue comment add ISSUE-1 "My comment body" --internal
```

### Move/Transition

Source: https://github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/blob/main/README.md

If your workflow allows to add comment, resolution or assignee while moving an issue, you can do so as shown below. See [this documentation](https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/how-to-add-a-comment-during-a-transition-779160682.html) on how to setup your workflow to allow these fields.
